message:
🌱 Add linkerd: Proxy returns 404 when HTTP connection is marked as opaque mission (#1612)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "linkerd-9888-proxy-returns-404-when-http-connection-is-marked-as-opaque",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "linkerd: Proxy returns 404 when HTTP connection is marked as opaque",
+    "description": "Proxy returns 404 when HTTP connection is marked as opaque. Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current linkerd deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your linkerd version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd\nhelm list -n linkerd 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working linkerd installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review linkerd configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd\nkubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd\n```\n### What is the issue?\n\nIf a HTTP is sent to a port which is marked as opaque, the proxy returns a 404 response with `no route found for request` as the error, even if no Server resource is defined for that port.\n\n### How can it be reproduced?\n\n1."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Proxy returns 404 when HTTP connection is marked as opaque",
+        "description": "One aspect of this that jumps out at me is the subtle difference between what we call \"opaque protocol\" and \"opaque transport\".\n\nOpaque protocol is enabled by setting the opaque ports annotation on the Service and instructs the outbound proxy to skip protocol detection.  Opaque transport is enabled\n```yaml\n# curl -v web-svc.emojivoto.svc.cluster.local\n[...]\n*   Trying 10.96.92.112...\n* TCP_NODELAY set\n* Expire in 200 ms for 4 (transfer 0x55fded8edf50)\n* Connected to web-svc.emojivoto.svc.cluster.local (10.96.92.112) port 80 (#0)\n> GET / HTTP/1.1\n> Host: web-svc.emojivoto.svc.cluster.local\n> User-Agent: curl/7.64.0\n> Accept: */*\n> \n< HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\n< l5d-proxy-error: no route found for request\n< date: Thu, 17 Nov 2022 22:53:06 GMT\n< content-length: 0\n<\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd\nkubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Proxy returns 404 when HTTP connection is marked as opaque\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "One aspect of this that jumps out at me is the subtle difference between what we call \"opaque protocol\" and \"opaque transport\".\n\nOpaque protocol is enabled by setting the opaque ports annotation on the Service and instructs the outbound proxy to skip protocol detection.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# curl -v web-svc.emojivoto.svc.cluster.local\n[...]\n*   Trying 10.96.92.112...\n* TCP_NODELAY set\n* Expire in 200 ms for 4 (transfer 0x55fded8edf50)\n* Connected to web-svc.emojivoto.svc.cluster.local (10.96.92.112) port 80 (#0)\n> GET / HTTP/1.1\n> Host: web-svc.emojivoto.svc.cluster.local\n> User-Agent: curl/7.64.0\n> Accept: */*\n> \n< HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\n< l5d-proxy-error: no route found for request\n< date: Thu, 17 Nov 2022 22:53:06 GMT\n< content-length: 0\n<",
+        "[    34.704855s] DEBUG ThreadId(01) inbound:accept{client.addr=10.244.125.138:51892}:server{port=4143}:direct: linkerd_tls::server: Peeked bytes from TCP stream sz=0\n[    34.704877s] DEBUG ThreadId(01) inbound:accept{client.addr=10.244.125.138:51892}:server{port=4143}:direct: linkerd_tls::server: Attempting to buffer TLS ClientHello after incomplete peek\n[    34.704881s] DEBUG ThreadId(01) inbound:accept{client.addr=10.244.125.138:51892}:server{port=4143}:direct: linkerd_tls::server: Reading bytes from TCP stream buf.capacity=8192\n[    34.704888s] DEBUG ThreadId(01) inbound:accept{client.addr=10.244.125.138:51892}:server{port=4143}:direct: linkerd_tls::server: Read bytes from TCP stream buf.len=288\n[    34.706722s] DEBUG ThreadId(01) inbound:accept{client.addr=10.244.125.138:51892}:server{"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "linkerd",
+      "graduated",
+      "networking",
+      "feature"
+    ],

+    },

+  },

+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",

+      "kubectl"
+    ],

+  },

+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-21T06:19:45.334Z",
